evidence.detail: process_content accepted modes extract, summarize, classify, translate, and embed in one description.
Summarization requests sometimes selected extract mode because the mode was not explicit in the tool contract.

tools.name: extract_text
tools.description: Extracts verbatim text from supplied content without summarizing or classifying it. Use when the user asks for exact source text, fields, or raw sections; for summaries use summarize_text instead.
tools.input_format: Plain text, markdown, HTML, or document text payload
example_queries: Extract every heading from this markdown document.
Return the raw clauses from this contract text.
tools.boundary: For summaries or labels use summarize_text or classify_text instead.

tools.name: summarize_text
tools.description: Creates a concise summary from supplied text while preserving stated facts. Use when the user asks for a short brief, abstract, or recap; for exact extraction use extract_text instead.
tools.input_format: Plain text or markdown text payload
example_queries: Summarize this transcript in five bullets.
Create a one paragraph abstract from this article.
tools.boundary: For exact source extraction use extract_text; for category labels use classify_text instead.

tools.name: classify_text
tools.description: Assigns one or more labels from a declared taxonomy to supplied text. Use when the request provides labels or asks for categorization; for summaries use summarize_text instead.
tools.input_format: Text payload plus explicit taxonomy or label set
example_queries: Classify these support tickets by priority.
Apply the provided compliance taxonomy to this policy excerpt.
tools.boundary: For summarization use summarize_text; for raw extraction use extract_text instead.

overlap_findings.evidence: One tool exposes multiple modes with different output contracts.
The original description never states when to choose extraction instead of summarization.

priority_actions.reason: Overloaded modes are the highest severity issue and require separate tool contracts.
Each split tool must state its neighboring boundary.

guardian.reason: The overloaded tool is split into single-purpose contracts with deterministic routing boundaries.
